description: 11 prompts that exercise the four core workflows plus the critic/variations side-channels. Each eval defines a prompt the agent receives, the file artifacts it should produce, and a list of assertions the grader can run on those artifacts. Assertions are written so they do not depend on a particular planner or wording — they check structural and behavioural outcomes.

agentReceives: the agent's normal tools (file edits, shell)
for skill runs: the full mermaid-floorplan skill (SKILL.md + references + scripts)
for baseline runs: only knowledge that the mermaid-floorplan DSL exists, with a pointer to mermaid-floorplan/examples/

evals.title: Create a small studio from a one-line brief
evals.prompt: Create a 480 sqft studio apartment as a .floorplan file. Save it as plan.floorplan. The studio should have a sleeping area, a kitchen, a bathroom, and an entry vestibule.

evals.title: Studio with separated wet rooms and no hallway
evals.prompt: Create a 480 sqft studio apartment as a .floorplan file. Save it as plan.floorplan. The studio should have a sleeping area, a kitchen, a bathroom, and a living/entry zone. Hard preferences: (1) the kitchen and the bathroom must NOT share a wall; (2) the bathroom door should NOT open onto the entry/foyer/outside (route it off the sleeping area instead); (3) do NOT include a dedicated hallway/corridor — the public zone should be open-plan. Also render to plan.png.

evals.title: 2BR apartment with the wet-wall constraint
evals.prompt: Design a 1000 sqft 2-bedroom apartment in the mermaid-floorplan DSL. Bedrooms should each have at least one window. The kitchen and bathroom MUST share a plumbing wall. Save as twobr.floorplan and render to twobr.png.

evals.title: 3BR house with ensuite + dedicated dining
evals.prompt: Lay out a 3-bedroom suburban house, around 1800 sqft, single floor. The master bedroom needs an ensuite bath. There should be a separate dining room and a hall bathroom for the other two bedrooms. Keep all bedrooms accessible via a hallway, not through other rooms. Save as house.floorplan and render to house.png.

evals.title: Rename + resize a few rooms in an existing plan
evals.prompt: Open the supplied two-br-seed.floorplan. Rename the room currently called Bedroom2 to KidsRoom, and shrink it from its current size to 10x12. Then increase the Master bedroom by 2 ft on its long edge. Save the result as plan.floorplan.

evals.title: Add a walk-in closet inside the master bedroom
evals.prompt: Add a walk-in closet (4x8 ft, kind 'closet') anchored to the right side of the Master bedroom in two-br-seed.floorplan. Save the result as with-closet.floorplan.

evals.title: Convert an absolute-positioned plan to relative positioning
evals.prompt: The supplied absolute-positions.floorplan uses `at (x, y)` for every room. Convert it to use relative positioning anchored at the Foyer. Save as relative.floorplan.

evals.title: Recover DSL from a clean rendered floorplan PNG
evals.prompt: The supplied source-image.png shows a single-floor apartment. Produce a .floorplan file (recovered.floorplan) that reproduces the layout as closely as possible. Render it to recovered.png and run compare_visual.py against the source.

evals.title: Honour low-confidence dimensions instead of hallucinating
evals.prompt: The supplied blurry-source.png is a phone photo of a hand-drawn floor plan; some dimensions are illegible. Produce both a program brief (brief.json) with explicit confidence scores and a derived recovered.floorplan. For any dimension you can't read, do NOT invent a number — use the brief's confidence field and let the generator pick a default.

assertions.rule: if size present without confidence, fail

evals.title: Three-turn natural-language refinement
evals.prompt: Apply three sequential edits to two-br-seed.floorplan: (1) make the kitchen 2 ft wider, (2) swap Bedroom2 and Master so Bedroom2 is on the right, (3) add a 4x4 powder room next to the Foyer. Save the final result as final.floorplan. After EACH turn, re-run validate.mjs and design_critic.mjs and bail out with an explanation if the plan becomes invalid.

evals.title: Three layout variations from a single brief
evals.prompt: Use generate_variations.mjs to produce 3 layout variations from twobr-brief.json (linear, L-shape, central-corridor). Save them to variations/ and render each to a PNG. All three must validate.

evals.title: Two-story 3BR townhouse with stair core + 3D feedback
evals.prompt: Design a 2-story 3-bedroom townhouse, ~1800 sqft total, in the mermaid-floorplan DSL. Public rooms (entry, living, kitchen, dining, powder) belong on the ground floor; bedrooms and baths on the first floor. The stair must be declared on BOTH floors and connected with a `vertical` link. Render BOTH a 2D plan (plan.png) AND an axonometric 3D view (plan-3d.png) using scripts/render_3d.mjs. Save the DSL as plan.floorplan.

evals.title: Fix an undersized stair core (stair_through_walls + landing rules)
evals.prompt: The supplied StairConstraints-rough.floorplan has a 6 x 14 ft StairCore that cannot fit a straight 10 ft residential stair (16.5 ft of run + 2 x 3 ft landings). Read the new "Stair footprint and landings" subsection in references/multi-floor.md, run design_critic.mjs and suggest_improvements.mjs, then either resize the StairCore (apply via modify.mjs) or switch the stair shape to U-shaped/L-shaped/spiral. Emit the corrected file as fixed.floorplan and verify with design_critic.mjs --strict.

evals.title: Fix a straight-stair dead-end landing (stair_landing_egress)
evals.prompt: The supplied StairLandingEgress-rough.floorplan has a correctly sized 6 x 23 ft StairCore for a straight 10 ft residential stair, but only the *arrival* (top-step) landing has a connection to the rest of each floor. The *boarding* (bottom-step) landing on the ground floor is stranded — anyone on the ground floor cannot reach the first tread. Run design_critic.mjs to confirm the new `stair_landing_egress` finding, then run suggest_improvements.mjs and apply the resulting `add_connection` op via modify.mjs. The auto-suggested connector is `opening` because `config.stair_code = residential`. Emit the corrected file as fixed.floorplan and verify with design_critic.mjs --strict.

evals.title: Improve a rough studio: drive critic findings to zero
evals.prompt: The supplied studio-rough.floorplan is a flawed studio. Use design_critic.mjs and suggest_improvements.mjs to identify the design issues, apply fixes via modify.mjs, and produce fixed.floorplan. The fixed plan should still be a studio (no extra bedrooms/walls of separation) but should resolve as many critic findings as possible.
